name: Grant County Bank
description_short: Grant County Bank is an FDIC-insured community bank offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and credit cards with online and mobile banking services.
description_long:
Grant County Bank is a full-service community bank that operates as an FDIC-insured financial institution backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank serves both personal and business customers through a network of physical locations and digital banking channels. Based on their website, they have established themselves as a traditional retail bank with comprehensive product offerings.

The bank provides a broad range of personal banking products including multiple checking account tiers (e-Free, Classic, and Secure Checking), savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, IRAs, HSAs, and personal loans. For business customers, Grant County Bank offers business checking, business savings, business money market accounts, business CDs, and specialized lending products including business/agriculture loans, commercial real estate loans, and lines of credit. They also issue both personal and business credit cards. Their lending portfolio extends to home mortgages, home equity loans, and auto loans.

Grant County Bank distinguishes itself through features like surcharge-free ATM access, mobile banking with mobile deposit capabilities, person-to-person (P2P) payments, online bill pay, and weekend customer support (Friday 6pm-8pm, Saturday 9am-8pm, Sunday 1pm-8pm). They offer wealth management services including trust services and investments, plus business services like ACH, merchant card services, and remote deposit capture. The bank's website emphasizes accessibility features and provides tools like financial calculators and account switching assistance.

As a community bank, Grant County Bank appeals to customers seeking traditional banking relationships with personal service options, though they compete with larger national banks on product breadth and with digital-native banks on convenience. Their specific rates, loan terms, and fee structures are not disclosed on the website, requiring direct contact for pricing details. The availability of surcharge-free ATMs and weekend help desk support are notable conveniences, though their geographic footprint appears limited to their specific branch locations. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with CDARS & ICS options
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and Health Savings Accounts (HSAs)
Home mortgage, home equity, and auto loans
Business/agriculture loans and commercial real estate loans
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government
Surcharge-free ATM network access for account holders
Weekend customer support hours (Fri 6pm-8pm, Sat 9am-8pm, Sun 1pm-8pm)
Multiple account tier options for both personal and business checking
Mobile banking with mobile deposit capability
Comprehensive loan products including mortgages, auto loans, and agriculture loans
Online account opening available for new customers
cons: Specific rates, fees, and loan terms not disclosed on website—requires contacting bank directly
Appears to be a regional/community bank with limited branch network compared to national banks
No mention of competitive rate guarantees or price matching on deposits or loans
best_for: Small business owners and agricultural customers seeking specialized lending and business services
Customers in Grant County or surrounding areas seeking local banking relationships
People who value weekend customer support availability and surcharge-free ATM access
diagnosis: Grant County Bank is best for personal and business customers seeking a full-service community bank with local branch relationships, specialized agriculture lending, and convenient digital services. The main caveat is that specific pricing (rates, fees, loan terms) is not available online and requires direct contact with the bank, making rate and fee comparison more time-intensive than with banks that publish this information publicly.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
